public_reputation: Known around Ashwake Port through day-to-day work as shrine attendant.
appearance: Rosal Sealwater carries the marks of shrine attendant in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Protects small rituals with quiet firmness and notices subtle breaches long before they become public offense. In Ashwake Port, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of shrine attendant in and around Ashwake Port, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Ashwake Port while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Rosal Sealwater can draw the party into a locality problem centered on shrine attendant duties in Ashwake Port.
Rosal Sealwater becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with harbor ash clogging, storm-whirlpool risk, port rivalry around Ashwake Port.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Rosal Sealwater is immediately readable as a shrine attendant tied to Ashwake Port.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Ashwake Port: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Rosal Sealwater presents as someone shaped by the duties of a shrine attendant and the daily pressures of Ashwake Port.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a shrine attendant, and proof that strangers will not make Ashwake Port harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Rosal Sealwater refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a shrine attendant too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can identify offerings, taboos, and recent signs of ritual irregularity.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Rosal Sealwater adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Rosal Sealwater always knows more about Ashwake Port than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a shrine attendant in Ashwake Port.
social_texture: Rosal Sealwater speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Ashwake Port, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Rosal touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Rosal Sealwater when the party needs a socially grounded face for Ashwake Port: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
